ORDER DENYNG PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO CONDITIONALY SEAL COMPLAINT AND MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND SUPPORTINGF DOCUMENTS.
MINUTES FOR MAR-12-2025 9:00 AM

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF LYDRASOFT, INC.'S MOTION TO CONDITIONALLY SEAL COMPLAINT, MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING RE-FILED. LYDRASOFT SEEKS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN TO DEFENDANT ATOS LIMITED TO IDENTIFY WHAT, IF ANY, DOCUMENTS SHOULD BE SEALED, YET LYDRASOFT HAS NOT SHOWN THAT IT HAS PERSONAL JURISDICTION OVER ATOS OR THAT ATOS HAS RECEIVED SUFFICIENT TIME TO RESPOND TO THIS MOTION OR THE COMPLAINT. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/HEK)

Off Calendar May-21-2025 Continued To Aug-20-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Preliminary Injunction; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities In Support Of Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Mar-12-2025 Text Ruling

MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Conditionally Seal Complaint, Motion For Preliminary Injunction And Supporting Documents; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities
Off Calendar Feb-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
ADRIAN BARNES, ESQ. / CHRISTINA TUSAN, ESQ, TUSAN LAW, P.C. (626) 418-8203 abarnes@ctusanlaw.com / ctusan@ctusanlaw.com
Attorneys for Plaintiff
No Other Appearances

Plaintiff did not properly contest the court's tentative ruling. Plaintiff's late notice of contest was received by email on Sunday February 23, 2024 at 11:00 p.m.

No argument is held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LYDRASOFT, INC.'S MOTION To Conditionally Seal Complaint, Motion For Preliminary Injunction

Plaintiff Lydrasoft, Inc.'s motion to seal documents is denied without prejudice to being re-filed. Lydrasoft seeks to shift the burden to defendant Atos Limited to identify what, if any, documents should be sealed, yet Lydrasoft has not shown that it has personal jurisdiction over Atos or that Atos has received sufficient time to respond to this motion or the complaint.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/HEK)
